⭕️IDF Inquiry Concludes: Yesterday’s incident in which @UNIFIL_ troops were killed was not caused by IDF activity.

Over the past day, the IDF completed its review of the incident yesterday, in which it was reported that UNIFIL troops were killed by the explosion of an explosive device in the Bani Haiyyan area in southern Lebanon. A comprehensive operational examination indicates that no explosive device was placed in the area by IDF troops, and that no IDF troops were present in the area at all.

The IDF is operating against Hezbollah, and not against UNIFIL, the Lebanese Armed Forces, or Lebanese civilians. 

The IDF calls on UNIFIL to avoid presence in combat zones where the IDF has issued warnings to the civilian population to evacuate for their safety.

The Council examined a series of presentations and security reports, alongside deliberations on institutional policies governing security and operational performance at the national level. These discussions were framed within ongoing efforts to enhance policing efficiency and further strengthen the national security ecosystem.

RT by @moiuae: UAE air defences engaged 8 Ballistic missiles and 4 Cruise missiles and 36 UAV's.

The UAE air defences on 31th March 2026 engaged 8 Ballistic missiles, 4 Cruise missiles and 36 UAV's launched from Iran.

Since the start of the blatant Iranian attacks, UAE air defences have engaged 433 ballistic missiles, 19 cruise missiles and 1,977 UAVs.

The attacks resulted in the martyrdom of 2 members of the armed forces while performing their national duty, in addition to the martyrdom of a Moroccan civilian contracted by the Armed Forces, as well as 8 fatalities of Pakistani, Nepali, Bangladeshi, Palestinian and Indian nationalities.

A total of 188 people were also injured, with injuries ranging from minor to moderate and severe. The injured included nationals of the UAE, Egypt, Sudan, Ethiopia, the Philippines, Pakistan, Iran, India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Azerbaijan, Yemen, Uganda, Eritrea, Lebanon, Afghanistan, Bahrain, Comoros, Türkiye, Iraq, Nepal, Nigeria, Oman, Jordan, Palestine, Ghana, Indonesia, Sweden and Tunisia.

The Ministry of Defence affirmed that it remains fully prepared and ready to deal with any threats and will firmly confront anything that aims to undermine the security of the country, in a manner that ensures the protection of its sovereignty, security and stability and safeguards its interests and national capabilities.

#وزارة_الدفاع 
#وزارة_الدفاع_الإماراتية
#MOD
#UAEMinistryOfDefence

For your information:

Due to the Passover holiday, the crossings from Israel into the Gaza Strip will be closed this coming Thursday (April 2) and on Wednesday (April 8). Despite the holiday, collection efforts will continue on the Gazan side of the Kerem Shalom Crossing on those days.

We would like to remind you that, despite the security situation, Israel continues to allow and facilitate the entry of hundreds of trucks into the Gaza Strip every day, in accordance with the needs identified by the UN and international organizations. Moreover, it should be emphasized that throughout the ceasefire, significant quantities of humanitarian aid entered the Gaza Strip, including food in volumes amounting to four times the estimated needs according to the UN's methodology.

The data on the ground, also supported by the CMCC, clearly indicates a significant, stable, and continuous volume of aid, as well as sufficient food availability in the Gaza Strip for an extended period.

It should be noted that on the other days during the coming two weeks, the crossings will operate as usual, and Israel will continue to allow and facilitate the humanitarian response for the population in the Gaza Strip, in cooperation with the UN, donor states, the private sector, and approved international organizations operating lawfully and in full cooperation with Israel.

⭕ The Chief of the General Staff concluded the inquiry into the conduct of IDF soldiers toward journalists while securing the area during the evacuation of an illegal outpost in Area A of Judea and Samaria.

The inquiry found several failures in the conduct of the soldiers toward the reporters. In addition, deficiencies were identified in behavioral norms, deviations from IDF orders, and inappropriate communication with members of the press in violation of established procedures. 

The IDF respects and enables freedom of the press in the area and regrets the incident, therefore there was an immediate suspension of the battalion’s operational deployment in the area. 

From the remarks of the Chief of the General Staff, LTG Eyal Zamir:
“This is a grave ethical incident that is out of line with IDF norms and values. We all swore the soldier’s oath upon enlistment—weapons are to be used solely for the purpose of carrying out the mission, and never for revenge. We will not accept such incidents within the ranks of the IDF.”

Summary of casualty evacuations from the missile launch toward the Be’er Sheva area:
MDA EMTs and paramedics provided medical treatment and evacuated 31 casualties to Soroka Hospital, including:

11 with minor injuries from objects displaced by the blast

20 with anxiety attacks

Summary of casualty evacuations from the missile launch toward the Be’er Sheva area: MDA EMTs and paramedics provided medical treatment and evacuated 31 casualties to Soroka Hospital, including: 11 with minor injuries from objects displaced by the blast 20 with anxiety attacks

Following the missile fire toward southern Israel:

In the Be’er Sheva region, MDA teams are providing medical treatment and evacuating 13 casualties to Soroka Hospital, including: 4 in mild condition who were injured by blast impacts, and 9 suffering from anxiety.

Following the missile fire toward southern Israel: In the Be’er Sheva region, MDA teams are providing medical treatment and evacuating 13 casualties to Soroka Hospital, including: 4 in mild condition who were injured by blast impacts, and 9 suffering from anxiety.

MDA Medicycle Unit EMT Hananel Benita:

“After the 101 call re missiles to the south, we responded with ambulances, MICUs, and medicycles. Despite heavy damage, no physical injuries were found. We treated several for anxiety and continue searching to ensure no more casualties.”

MDA Medicycle Unit EMT Hananel Benita: “After the 101 call re missiles to the south, we responded with ambulances, MICUs, and medicycles. Despite heavy damage, no physical injuries were found. We treated several for anxiety and continue searching to ensure no more casualties.”

MDA Spokesperson: Following the missile fire toward southern Israel:

In the Be’er Sheva region, MDA EMTs and paramedics are treating several anxiety casualties at the scene, and are continuing to search locations where reports were received. Updates as required.

While @UNRWA focuses on false narratives to try and stay relevant, we focus on the facts of Gaza’s water supply:

💧 Over 70,000 m³ of water facilitated daily.
💧 4 active pipelines: Nahal Oz, Bani Suheila, Birkat Sa'id, & the Emirati line.
💧 Desalination plants and wells operational through our coordination.

​The facts speak for themselves: when a water line issue was reported last week, we coordinated a rapid repair to restore full functionality immediately.

​While local groundwater faces challenges, we continue to repair infrastructure, even during combat, to ensure civilians access to safe, potable water.
